solutions.solution: (d) 5 4 6 8 3 9 → X A F K • X. In place of B (condition I) is applied. ∴ 546839 ⇒ XAF•K•X
(c) Here, none of the condition is applied, so the coding is done as follows N U B A Q E → 3 6 ! $ @ #. ∴ Code for NUBAQE ⇒ 36!$@#

(d) When no condition is applied, the coding is done as follows F W Z O R H → 9 δ δ @ ∞ ©. But here the second and third letters are consonants, therefore condition (iii) is applied here. As condition (iii) is applied here, both the second and third letters are to be coded as the code for the third letter. F W Z O R H → 9 δ δ @ ∞ ©. ∴ Code for FWZORH ⇒ 9δδ@∞©

(d) When no condition is applied, the coding is done as follows M B I U H Q → % 2 η 6 ◊ 4. But here the first letter is a consonant and the third letter is a vowel, therefore condition (i) is applied here. As condition (i) is applied here, the codes for first and third letters are interchanged. M B I U H Q → η 2 % 6 ◊ 4. ∴ Code for MBIUHQ ⇒ η 2%6◊4
(b) Here, none of the condition is applied, so the coding is done as follows O P A I H M → @ ★ $ η ℧ %. ∴ Code for OPAIHM ⇒ @★$ η ℧ %

(d) When no condition is applied, the coding is done as follows E A M W B N → # $ % δ 2 3. But here the first letter is a vowel and the fourth letter is a consonant, therefore condition (ii) is applied here. As condition (ii) is applied, both the first and the fourth letters are to be coded as the code for the vowel. E A M W B N → # $ % # 2 3. ∴ Code for EAMWBN ⇒ #$%#23
(e) Here, none of the condition is applied, so the coding is done as follows A I W E Z B → $ η δ # ℧ 2. ∴ Code for AIWEZB ⇒ $ η δ # ℧ 2
